_meta.description: Known MCA (Merchant Cash Advance) funders and their legal entities. Used to identify MCA-related UCC-1 filings. Tier 1 = Pure MCA, Tier 2 = MCA + Other Lending, Tier 3 = Adjacent Alternative Lending.
_meta.maintainer_note: Add new funders as they are discovered. Update DBAs when funders rebrand. Verify tiers quarterly.

funders.notes: One of the largest MCA funders. Frequent UCC filer.
Major MCA funder, also does SBA. Very active UCC filer.
Large MCA funder, frequent UCC filer.
Major MCA/alternative lender. Formerly Strategic Funding Source.
Large alt lender, does MCA and term loans.
Pure MCA funder, active UCC filer.
MCA funder, part of the Everest group.
Well-known MCA funder. Very active in UCC filings.
Major MCA funder (renamed/acquired by Kapitus). Historical filings may still show.
Online small business lending platform, MCA + SBA.
Invoice factoring + MCA for small businesses.
Online small business lender, MCA + line of credit.
MCA funder focused on small advances.
SBA lender that also funds MCA through partners. Utah-based industrial bank.
Issues MCA/lending through partners (PayPal, Affirm, etc.).
Bank that partners with many fintech/MCA companies.
Regional bank that does MCA/SBA lending.
Commercial bank that does some alt lending.
Commercial bank with small business lending.
Online bank that partners with MCA funders.
Florida bank that funds some MCA.
Commercial bank (acquired by PNC, but legacy filings exist).
Issues prepaid cards and partners with fintech/MCA firms.
Equipment finance, factoring, and some MCA.
Large commercial bank with asset-based lending.
Midwest bank with equipment finance division.
Regional bank with small business lending.
Equipment finance and small business lending.
Equipment finance — adjacent to MCA market.
Square/Block's MCA arm — files UCCs for Square Loans.
Small business term loans — files UCCs.
Consumer lending platform (Cross River Bank partner).
Corporate card + spend management (acquired by Bill.com). Files UCCs.
Amex small business loans — files UCCs.
Corporate card with working capital — files UCCs.
Small business lending — MCA and term loans.
MCA funder focused on women-owned businesses.
MCA funder (not the asset manager BlackRock).
MCA funder based in NY area.
